info.name: CustomBond API
info.description:
Complete Postman collection for the CustomBond application.

## Setup
1. Import `CustomBond.postman_environment.json` as your environment.
2. Set `base_url` to match your deployment (default: `http://localhost:8075/api`).
3. Run **DXC Auth → Login** first – the test script auto-saves the token to the `dxc_token` environment variable.

## Controllers
| Controller | Base Path | Description |
|---|---|---|
| DxcAuthController | `/dxc/auth` | DXC authentication (login / logout) |
| DxcController | `/dxc/services` | Individual DXC service calls |
| CBIssueInsurancePolicyController | `/cb/services` | CB full-cycle issuance (legacy) |
| VendorController | `/vendor` | Async vendor pipeline – main entry point |

## Vendor Pipeline Flow
The recommended entry point for vendors is `POST /vendor/issue`.
The server responds immediately with HTTP 202 and a `requestId`.
The configured steps execute asynchronously. Results are logged and
optionally POSTed to your `callbackUrl`.

item.name: DXC Auth
item.description: Authentication endpoints. Run Login first – the test script captures the token automatically.

exec: // Persist token for all subsequent requests

request.description:
Authenticates with the DXC system and returns a Bearer token.
The test script automatically stores the token in the `dxc_token` environment variable.
Invalidates the current DXC session token.

item.name: DXC Services
item.description:
Individual DXC service endpoints – each maps directly to one external DXC API call.
All requests require the DXC token (auto-set by the Login request).

body.raw:
{
  "callSequance": "FIRST_CALL",
  "stakeholderName": "Acme Corporation",
  "sectorType": "PRIVATE",
  "vendorId": "{{vendor_id}}",
  "taxId": "123456789",
  "nationalId": null,
  "contactKey": null,
  "commercialRegNo": "CR-2024-001",
  "address": "123 Business District, Cairo",
  "phone": "+201234567890",
  "email": "contact@acme.com",
  "legalRepName": "Mohammed Ahmed",
  "legalRepNationalId": "12345678901234",
  "vendorRequestId": "{{vendor_request_id}}"
}

request.description:
Checks whether a stakeholder (identified by taxId, nationalId, or contactKey) is blacklisted.

**callSequance values:** `FIRST_CALL` | `SECOND_CALL`
**sectorType values:** `PRIVATE` | `PUBLIC`

At least one of `taxId`, `nationalId`, or `contactKey` is required.

**Response status field:** `APPROVED` | `REJECTED`
Variant: identify the stakeholder by `nationalId` (14-digit Egyptian national ID).
Variant: identify the stakeholder by DXC `contactKey` (integer).

exec: pm.test('Response contains policyNo and policyKey', function () {
// Save for use in Issue Quote and Issue Policy

request.description:
Prepares a new policy quote. Returns `policyNo` and `policyKey`.

The test script auto-saves both values to environment variables for downstream calls.

**Response shape:**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Policy issued successfully",
  "data": {
    "policyKey": 12345,
    "policyNo": "POL-2024-001"
  }
}
```

**Note:** `currency` uses ISO 4217 numeric codes (818 = EGP).

Issues the quote for a policy that was prepared via Quote Preparation.

**Prerequisites:** Run Quote Preparation first – the test script saves `{{policy_no}}`.

`parentRequestId` links all log entries for a single issuance flow.

Finalises and issues the insurance policy.

**Prerequisites:** Run Issue Quote first.

This is the third and final step of the DXC policy lifecycle.

formdata.description: The document binary to upload (PDF, PNG, JPG, etc.)
Upload metadata as a JSON string

request.description:
Uploads a document and attaches it to a policy.

**Multipart form fields:**
- `File` – the document binary
- `Data` – JSON string with upload metadata (Content-Type: application/json)

`instnceKey` corresponds to the `policyKey` returned by Quote Preparation.

`documentType` is a DXC reference code for the document category.

Retrieves a previously uploaded document from the database by its upload `requestId`.

`requestId` is the UUID logged during the Upload Document call.

Retrieves a DXC contact record by Tax ID.

Provide exactly one of `taxId`, `nationalId`, or `contactKey`.
Retrieves a DXC contact record by 14-digit Egyptian National ID.
Retrieves a DXC contact record by its integer contact key.

Creates a new individual (person) contact in the DXC system.

Set `individualContactData` and leave `organizationContactData` null.

Common `country` values: 818 = Egypt.
Common `gender` values: 1 = Male, 2 = Female.

body.raw:
{
  "callSequance": "FIRST_CALL",
  "vendorId": "{{vendor_id}}",
  "vendorRequestId": "{{vendor_request_id}}",
  "NafazaRequestId": null,
  "parentRequestId": "{{parent_request_id}}",
  "organizationContactData": {
    "companyName": "Acme Corporation",
    "taxCardId": "123456789",
    "commercialRegID": "CR-2024-001",
    "typeOfTax": 1,
    "sector": 2,
    "country": 818,
    "mobilePhone": "+20212345678",
    "email": "info@acme.com",
    "address1": "456 Business Tower",
    "address2": "Downtown Cairo",
    "city": "Cairo",
    "state": 1,
    "niprNumber": null,
    "niprExpirationDate": null,
    "roles": [
      { "role": 2 }
    ]
  }
}

request.description:
Creates a new organization (company) contact in the DXC system.

Set `organizationContactData` and leave `individualContactData` null.

`taxCardId` must be a valid 9-digit Egyptian Tax Registration Number.

item.name: CB Services (Legacy Full Cycle)
item.description: CBIssueInsurancePolicyController – legacy endpoint that triggers the full CB policy issuance cycle asynchronously. Replaced by the more flexible Vendor Pipeline API (`/vendor/issue`).

request.description:
Triggers a full asynchronous CB issuance cycle including black-list check.
Returns a response immediately with `nafezaInsuranceRequestId`, `requestId`, and `acceptedAt`.

**Deprecated in favour of** `POST /vendor/issue` which supports per-vendor step configuration.

item.name: Vendor Pipeline API
item.description:
Main entry point for vendor-initiated issuance flows.

`POST /vendor/issue` accepts the request as `multipart/form-data`:
- Part **`data`** (Content-Type: application/json) – the `VendorIssuanceRequest` JSON
- Part **`file`** (optional binary) – required only when `UPLOAD_DOCUMENT` is in the `steps` list

The server responds immediately with HTTP **202 Accepted** and a `requestId` (UUID).
The configured pipeline steps then execute **asynchronously** on the server.

### Available step types
| Step | Requires block | Requires file |
|---|---|---|
| `CHECK_BLACK_LIST` | `blackList` | No |
| `QUOTE_PREPARATION` | `quotePreparation` | No |
| `UPLOAD_DOCUMENT` | `upload` | **Yes** |
| `ISSUE_QUOTE` | – (uses context policyNo) | No |
| `ISSUE_POLICY` | – (uses context policyNo) | No |

For `ISSUE_QUOTE` / `ISSUE_POLICY` without a preceding `QUOTE_PREPARATION`, supply `policyNo` at the top level.

For `UPLOAD_DOCUMENT` without a preceding `QUOTE_PREPARATION`, supply `upload.instnceKey` explicitly.

exec: pm.test('Steps list is echoed back', function () {

formdata.value:
{
  "vendorId": "{{vendor_id}}",
  "vendorRequestId": "{{vendor_request_id}}",
  "callbackUrl": "https://vendor.example.com/webhook/result",
  "steps": [
    "CHECK_BLACK_LIST",
    "QUOTE_PREPARATION",
    "UPLOAD_DOCUMENT",
    "ISSUE_QUOTE",
    "ISSUE_POLICY"
  ],

    "stakeholderName": "Acme Corporation",
    "sectorType": "PRIVATE",
    "taxId": "123456789",

    "contactKey": null,

  "quotePreparation": {
    "effectiveDate": "2024-07-01",
    "expiryDate": "2025-07-01",
    "currency": 818,
    "division": 1001,
    "subDivision": 2001,
    "insured": 12345,
    "underWriterContactKey": 67890,
    "mop": 1,
    "producer": 100,
    "maximumExposure": 500000,
    "product": 3001,
    "hazardLevel": 2,
    "policyName": 5,
    "contactTPA": 200,
    "gracePeriodDays": 30,
    "marketSourceKey": 10,
    "usePerecntageInBeneficiary": false,
    "benficiaries": [
      {
        "contactId": 11111,
        "limit": 100000,
        "currency": 818,
        "sharePercentage": 100,
        "print": true
      }
    ],
    "additionalInsureds": [
      {
        "contactId": 22222,
        "limit": 50000,
        "currency": 818
      }
    ]
  },
  "upload": {
    "entityKey": 10,
    "documentType": 3,
    "instnceKey": null
  }
}
formdata.description: VendorIssuanceRequest – full pipeline with all 5 steps
Document binary (required because UPLOAD_DOCUMENT is in the steps list)

request.description:
Runs all 5 steps in sequence:
1. CHECK_BLACK_LIST
2. QUOTE_PREPARATION (produces policyNo + policyKey)
3. UPLOAD_DOCUMENT (uses policyKey from step 2; `upload.instnceKey` is null so it's auto-resolved)
4. ISSUE_QUOTE (uses policyNo from step 2)
5. ISSUE_POLICY (uses policyNo from step 2)

The test script saves the returned `requestId` to `{{parent_request_id}}`.

**Important:** Attach a real document file in the `file` part before sending.

item.name: Issue – Quote + Issue Quote + Issue Policy (no document)

formdata.description: 3-step pipeline: prepare quote → issue quote → issue policy

request.description:
Vendor subset: Quote Preparation → Issue Quote → Issue Policy.
No file needed (UPLOAD_DOCUMENT is excluded).
No blackList check (CHECK_BLACK_LIST is excluded).

item.name: Issue – Black List + Issue Policy (pre-known policyNo)

formdata.description: 2-step pipeline: check blacklist then issue a pre-prepared policy

request.description:
Vendor subset: only CHECK_BLACK_LIST + ISSUE_POLICY.
No QUOTE_PREPARATION – the vendor supplies `policyNo` directly at the top level.
No document upload.

item.name: Issue – Issue Quote + Issue Policy (policyNo already known)

formdata.description: Minimal 2-step pipeline when policyNo is already available

request.description:
Vendor already has a `policyNo` (e.g. Quote Preparation was done externally).
Only Issue Quote and Issue Policy need to run.

formdata.description: Single-step pipeline: upload a document to an existing policy

request.description:
Single-step pipeline for uploading a document to an existing policy.
`upload.instnceKey` is supplied explicitly because QUOTE_PREPARATION is not in the pipeline.

Attach the document binary in the `file` part.

formdata.description: Single-step pipeline: only perform a blacklist check

request.description: Single-step pipeline – useful for vendors that only need the black-list check and handle the rest of the flow themselves.
